🔍 Small Event — Remote Eligibility Check
This show has 0 booths. Events at this size may be eligible for fully remote, hybrid, or fully on-site staffing. Answer the three questions below to receive a recommendation.
MHAs
lbs
$

📞 Fully Remote

Based on your inputs, this event is eligible for fully remote staffing. ES will not be on-site but will support customers remotely throughout the event. Follow the “Remote Events” section in the Preparing for an Event chapter of the Exhibitor Services Manual for next steps including exhibitor communications, Operations coordination, and service plan setup.

⏲ Partially On-Site (Hybrid)

Based on your inputs, this event is eligible for partially on-site (hybrid) staffing. ES will be present during certain peak times and support customers remotely for the remainder. Review the additional considerations below before finalizing this approach.

🏢 Fully On-Site

Based on your inputs, this event requires fully on-site staffing. One or more thresholds exceed remote eligibility criteria. Follow the next steps in the Preparing for an Event chapter of the Exhibitor Services Manual for an on-site staffed event.

Additional Considerations Before Finalizing

Staffing Role Definitions
ES Staffing Model — Role Reference
Customer-Facing Roles
ES team members working directly with exhibitors on the show floor and at the Exhibitor Service Center - answering questions, processing orders, and resolving service issues.
Back Room Roles
ES team members supporting back-of-house operations - coordinating with Operations, billing and auditing freight paperwork and labor tickets, and providing operational support to the customer-facing teams. Triggered on events when specific revenue thresholds are hit.
Additional Support Roles
Flex roles to support both customer-facing and back room needs during peak periods. Included in the model baseline for larger shows (601+ booths) where demand variability is higher.
Supervisory Roles
On-site leadership for the ES team — managing team performance, handling escalations that require leadership intervention, and serving as the primary point of contact for show management. Triggered at 451+ booths or $2M+ in estimated exhibitor revenue.
Freight Rigging / Storage Desk Roles
Roles dedicated to managing cold and frozen storage, access storage, or freight rigging desks. These are not driven by the staffing model — they default to 0 and should only be added if the show operationally requires a dedicated storage or rigging presence.

About These Roles

Customer-Facing Roles
The primary on-site position — scheduled for events of all sizes. These team members rotate between walking assigned sections of the show floor with mobile technology to proactively support exhibitors, and staffing the Exhibitor Service Center where customers place orders, ask billing questions, check freight status, and get help with last-minute needs. This is the core role always staffed first.
Back Room Roles
Support roles that operate behind the Exhibitor Service Center, not directly on the show floor. Back room team members handle administrative work such as completing labor tickets, processing freight, auditing paperwork, and helping track and coordinate service issue resolution using internal tools. They act as the connective tissue between front-line staff and operational execution.
Additional Support Roles
Supplemental staffing added at larger or more complex events when the standard team needs reinforcement. These roles are situational — deployed for time-bound periods such as peak move-in, or for events with heightened complexity, collection needs, or compressed timelines. Additional support provides flex capacity that allows the core team to maintain service levels when demand spikes.
Supervisory Roles
Event-level leadership for larger shows that require dedicated oversight of the ES team. This role ensures coordination across customer-facing, back room, and support staff; escalates complex issues; aligns with Operations and other partners; and owns the overall ES experience at the event. At some shows this role is filled by an Event Experience Supervisor (EES); at others, an ES Manager or Director takes on this function on-site.
Freight Rigging / Storage Desk Roles
Dedicated staffing for specialized service desks that manage specific freight or storage functions — such as cold storage, access-controlled storage, or freight rigging coordination. These needs are operationally determined on a show-by-show basis and are not driven by booth count or revenue. The model always defaults this to zero; the staffing leader adds roles when the event's setup is known to require them.
